failure_modes.text: Creeping determinism makes the causal path to a known outcome appear clear and necessary in hindsight, collapsing the genuine alternative paths that were equally plausible before the event.
failure_modes.mitigation: Reconstruct the genuine uncertainty at the time of the decision: creeping determinism makes the outcome look inevitable only after the fact.

failure_modes.text: Decision-quality contamination conflates outcome quality with decision quality — good processes with bad outcomes are penalized while bad processes with lucky outcomes are rewarded, corrupting the feedback signal for improving judgment.
failure_modes.mitigation: Evaluate decision quality separately from outcome quality: good processes with bad outcomes should not be penalized, and bad processes with good outcomes should not be rewarded.

failure_modes.text: False lesson extraction generates post-mortem lessons calibrated to the specific failure path as if it were uniquely predictable, producing 'fight the last war' defenses rather than improved probabilistic reasoning.
failure_modes.mitigation: Anchor post-mortem lessons to the decision process rather than to the specific failure path; false lesson extraction generates rules calibrated to one outcome.

premortem_questions.text: What does our contemporaneous documentation show we thought and what we were uncertain about at decision time — and how does that differ from what we now remember thinking?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Require contemporaneous documentation of decision rationale, uncertainty, and known alternatives

premortem_questions.text: What was the realistic base-rate probability of this outcome at decision time, based on comparable cases — and does that change our evaluation of the decision?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What was the realistic probability of this outcome at the time the decision was made?

premortem_questions.text: What were the three most plausible outcomes at decision time — and why do we now find it hard to remember them as equally plausible?
premortem_questions.source_quote: When learning from past failures, explicitly reconstruct the alternative paths that were live at the time

heuristics.text: Separate process evaluation from outcome evaluation: ask 'was this a good decision given what was known at the time?' before asking 'what happened?' — the sequence matters.
heuristics.source_quote: Separate process evaluation from outcome evaluation

heuristics.text: Require contemporaneous documentation of decision reasoning and uncertainty at decision time — use those documents in post-event review rather than relying on reconstructed memory.
heuristics.source_quote: Document Pre-Decision Reasoning: the only reliable antidote is timestamped records

heuristics.text: Reconstruct the alternative futures that were live at decision time — force the question of what could reasonably have happened instead, to restore the probability branching that hindsight collapsed.
heuristics.source_quote: Explicitly reconstruct the alternative paths that were live at the time of the decision

curation_notes.summary: Wave 2 for hindsight bias focuses on the three primary distortion mechanisms — creeping determinism, decision-quality contamination, false lesson extraction — with documentation-anchored and process-separating heuristics.
